qa_pairs.question: How do I ensure DPM source terms are updated every flow iteration in Fluent?
qa_pairs.answer: To ensure DPM source terms are updated every flow iteration, open the Discrete Phase Model Dialog Box and select 'Interaction with Continuous Phase'. This setting can be saved in your case file or added via the journal file.

qa_pairs.question: What journal file commands can automate DPM injection and interaction settings?
qa_pairs.answer: After solution initialization, you can automate DPM settings in the journal file with commands such as: `/define/models/dpm/interaction/coupled-calculations yes`, `/define/models/dpm/injections/delete-injection injection-0:1`, `/define/models/dpm/injections/create injection-0:1 no yes file no zinjection01.inj no no no no`, `/define/models/dpm/injections/list-particles injection-0:1`, and `/define/models/dpm/injections/list-injection-properties injection-0:1`.

qa_pairs.question: How can I confirm that a DPM injection is working in the Fluent GUI?
qa_pairs.answer: After specifying the injection and returning to the 'Injections' dialog box, you should see the specified Injection name. You can then list its Particles and Properties in the console to confirm it's working.

qa_pairs.question: What is the purpose of selecting 'Interaction with Continuous Phase' in the Discrete Phase Model Dialog Box?
qa_pairs.answer: Selecting 'Interaction with Continuous Phase' in the Discrete Phase Model Dialog Box enables the updating of Discrete Phase Model (DPM) source terms with every flow iteration.
